articles.title: AWKWARD MEETING BOT
articles.summary: Transcription tools are great, but they don't know what to focus on. Granola [4] follows your lead. It listens in on every call — without the awkward meeting bot — and then enhances the notes you've already written with useful context.

articles.title: PRODUCT COACHING AND AI
articles.summary: Product coaching is the biggest missing ingredient in most organizations. AI can now provide scalable, always-available coaching for product creators, while humans focus on leadership-level transformation.

articles.title: WHAT HAPPENS WHEN AGENTS MEET?
articles.summary: AI agents create system-wide effects when they operate at scale. Designing them requires population-level thinking, not just better individual interfaces.

articles.title: HARD TRUTHS FOR B2B IN 2026
articles.summary: If AI doesn't re-accelerate revenue, it doesn't matter. Agents are creating a winner-take-most dynamic that's crushing the middle of B2B SaaS.

articles.title: SHOULD TEAMS USE THE SAME TRACKER?
articles.summary: Transparency matters more than shared tooling. When trust drops, teams get managed through proxies, and delivery suffers.

articles.title: POWERFUL INSIGHTS FOR POWERFUL BRANDS!
articles.summary: Unwrap's customer intelligence platform [10] turns all your customer feedback (surveys, reviews, tickets, and social comments) into one clear view, using AI + NLP to surface the insights that matter most. Teams at Stripe, DoorDash, Clay, and WHOOP use Unwrap to cut through noise, track sentiment in real time, and make faster, data-backed product decisions.

articles.title: Book time to chat with the team.
THE AI DELEGATION MATRIX: WHAT PARTS OF YOUR UI SHOULDN'T EXIST?
articles.summary: Designing with AI is deciding who is accountable at each moment. Use a repeatable score for risk and payoff to choose between human control, AI assistance with approvals, or constrained autonomy.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD AN AI NATIVE PM OPERATING SYSTEM (61 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: AI-native PMs win by building an operating system, not a tool stack. Centralized context and MCP-powered workflows eliminate friction and unlock new leverage.

articles.title: PROGRAMMING WITH AI, WITHOUT THE HYPE
articles.summary: Programming with AI works best when treated as a tool, not a philosophy. Its real value comes from pairing it with strong context, clear planning, and human judgment.

articles.title: FRAGMENTS
articles.summary: AI should help humans find answers, not pretend to be one. As coding gets cheaper, judgment, incentives, and civic responsibility become the real constraints.

articles.title: SAYING “NO” IN AN AGE OF ABUNDANCE
articles.summary: AI removes building constraints, not user constraints.

articles.title: WHAT, THEN, ARE WE PAYING FOR?
articles.summary: Generative AI makes building software easy, but owning the underlying problem remains hard.
